data.title: Synopsys beats Q4 earnings forecast, EPS at $2.90 vs $2.78 expected. Revenue at $2.26B vs $2.25B estimate. CEO optimistic. FY 2026 outlook strong.
data.description: Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS) exceeded market expectations in its fourth-quarter earnings report. The company announced earnings of $2.90 per share, surpassing the projected $2.78. Quarterly revenue totaled $2.26 billion, slightly higher than the estimated $2.25 billion. Synopsys' CEO, Sassine Ghazi, expressed satisfaction with the results, highlighting the company's evolution into a leading provider of engineering solutions. Looking ahead, Synopsys anticipates adjusted earnings per share for fiscal 2026 to range between $14.32 and $14.40, surpassing the analyst prediction of $12.85. Additionally, the company expects fiscal revenue to fall within the range of $9.56 billion to $9.66 billion. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: PGP advises Dermatology Specialists of Omaha in partnership with Forefront Dermatology, 22nd group since 2018. - Chicago, Dec. 11, 2025.
data.description: Physician Growth Partners ("PGP") has successfully advised its 22nd dermatology group transaction since 2018 in Chicago. This highlights the firm's proficiency in facilitating successful mergers and acquisitions for dermatology and aesthetics practices.

data.title: Kuwait's automotive remanufacturing market is worth $1.2 billion, with growing demand for cost-effective and sustainable solutions.
data.description: A recent report on the Kuwait Automotive Remanufacturing market, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, reveals that the market is currently valued at USD 1.2 billion. The report analyzes data from the past five years and forecasts trends up to 2030. The growth in this market is attributed to rising interest in affordable and environmentally-friendly automotive options. This includes remanufacturing engines, alternators, and turbos. The report provides insights on market size, share, growth drivers, trends, opportunities, and competitive landscape.

data.title: Mitsubishi HC Capital Canada predicts stable industrial outlook for 2026; highlights key trends shaping Canadian sector.
data.description: Mitsubishi HC Capital Canada predicts four key trends shaping Canada's industrial equipment sector in 2026. The sector is affected by cautious investment strategies, technology-driven transformation, and evolving trade dynamics due to global supply chain realignments and automation adoption. Manufacturers are investing in robotics and AI to address workforce gaps, while forklift/material handling companies are upgrading equipment for efficiency. Logistic firms are exploring warehouse automation and electrification to keep up with environmental regulations. Labor shortages are driving automation and upskilling, leading to a shift towards technology and efficiency in the industry. Mitsubishi advises companies to use flexible financing and innovative strategies for sustainable growth.

data.title: Manifest MedEx partners with NinePatch to launch social health information exchange in California, integrating SDOH info.
data.description: In Riverside, California and Colorado Springs, Colorado, Manifest MedEx (MX), the largest nonprofit health information organization in California, has partnered with NinePatch®, a top SaaS platform for Whole Person Health and Referral Information Exchange. They have announced a collaboration to introduce a statewide social health information exchange (SHIE) platform. This platform will integrate social determinants of health (SDOH) information and tools, including care assessments, program enrollment, closed-loop referrals, and consent. This initiative aims to combine vital social health data to enhance healthcare services.

data.title: Chewy Inc. beats Q3 earnings expectations with adjusted earnings of 32 cents/share and sales of $3.117 billion. CEO optimistic.
data.description: Chewy Inc. (NYSE: CHWY) exceeded expectations with their third-quarter 2025 earnings report. Adjusted earnings per share were reported at 32 cents, surpassing both the consensus estimate of 13 cents and falling within the predicted range of 28-33 cents as provided by management. The company, known for its pet supplies, showcased a revenue of $3.117 billion for the quarter, indicating an 8.3% increase compared to the previous year. This figure also outperformed the consensus of $3.099 billion and aligned closely with the management projection of $3.07 billion to $3.1 billion. CEO Sumit Singh stated that Chewy continues to excel in the pet industry, expanding market share while demonstrating profit growth that exceeds sales growth. data.title: New Dickey’s Barbecue Pit opens in Littleton, Colorado with grand opening on December 13 featuring great meals, deals, and community support.
data.description: Littleton, Colorado has welcomed a new addition with the opening of Dickey’s Barbecue Pit. This latest location is looking to make a smoky impression, offering a variety of delicious meals to the community. The grand opening event on Saturday, December 13, promises a day filled with special deals and a chance to support a charitable cause. From 3:00 pm to 6:00 pm, there will be a live broadcast by Mike Boyle, adding to the festive atmosphere. Don't miss out on this opportunity to enjoy great food and give back to the community.

data.title: commercetools and Stripe partnership aids enterprises in AI-driven commerce, for faster sales via AI agents. BOSTON, Dec. 11, 2025 - PRNewswire.
data.description: A recent partnership has been established in Boston on December 11, 2025, aiming to streamline the process of discovering agents, facilitating checkouts, and managing payments for enterprises. This collaboration allows businesses to generate additional revenue through a unified integration. The use of AI in commerce is rapidly emerging as a significant avenue for growth, with millions of opportunities arising in this sector.

data.title: Roche's oral drug for breast cancer reduces recurrence by 30% in early-stage patients. Positive study results presented at 2025 Symposium.
data.description: Roche Holdings AG reported data from the phase 3 lidERA Breast Cancer study, which investigated giredestrant as adjuvant endocrine therapy for ER-positive, HER2-negative, early-stage breast cancer. The findings were presented at the 2025 San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium. According to a press release on Wednesday, Roche stated that Giredestrant is the first oral SERD to exhibit superior iDFS in the adjuvant setting. LidERA marks the second positive phase 3 outcome for giredestrant, following the evERA Breast Cancer results. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Rivian debuts custom silicon, next-gen autonomy platform, and deep AI integration at Autonomy & AI Day in Palo Alto.
data.description: Rivian made headlines by revealing major advancements in automotive technology during its Autonomy & AI Day held in Palo Alto. The company, listed on NASDAQ as RIVN, introduced its own specialized silicon and presented its plans for enhancing vehicle autonomy in the future. Additionally, Rivian showcased a new software structure that leverages artificial intelligence. The event attracted attention for the innovative developments announced, setting the stage for the company's next phase of growth and technology integration.

data.title: NDF achieves significant international milestones with over USD 40 billion in assistance in 50 years.
data.description: The Minister of Tourism and Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Saudi Fund for Development has officially opened the third day of the MOMENTUM 2025 Development Finance Conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The event took place on December 11, 2025.

data.title: BPCE and Generali have decided to halt negotiations about a Joint Venture in Asset Management.
data.description: BPCE and Generali have decided to discontinue discussions regarding their plans to create a Joint Venture in Asset Management. The collaboration, which aimed to enhance their asset management offerings, has been called off by mutual agreement. This decision comes after thorough deliberation, with both parties opting to cease negotiations. The establishment of a Joint Venture was expected to combine BPCE's assets of approximately $500 billion with Generali's assets under management of around $600 billion. Despite initial intentions, the two organizations have opted to end the talks without proceeding with the proposed venture.
